The branch refreshes stale managed `@openclaw/codex` runtime plugin installs through the trusted configured-plugin repair path and maps `codex/gpt-*` Codex-harness status usage/auth lookup through `openai-codex`.

PR surface: Source +80, Tests +246. Total +326 across 4 files.

target.title: fix(codex): refresh stale managed runtime plugin
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes the Codex stale-plugin convergence path behind https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87650, including the duplicate evidence from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88120.

What changes:

- Refreshes stale version-bound managed `@openclaw/codex` runtime packages through the trusted configured-plugin repair path when the installed package version is older than the running OpenClaw version.
- Leaves newer managed Codex packages alone so doctor does not downgrade a local/future install.
- Restores `/status` usage lookup for bare `codex/gpt-*` routes by mapping Codex harness status auth through `openai-codex`.

## Linked context

Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87650.

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88120. ClawSweeper closed that issue as a duplicate and asked that its stale-plugin-version, providerIds, config-backup, screenshot, and `codex/gpt-*` status evidence be folded into #87650.

Related but separate: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87851 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87436. That PR handles logical OpenAI session-route persistence after Codex-backed runs. This PR does not touch that persistence surface and is not intended as the repair for issue #87436. The changes are complementary: #87851 keeps durable session route state canonical, while this PR repairs stale managed Codex runtime package installs and the `codex/gpt-*` status/auth display path.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: stale managed `@openclaw/codex` packages could survive an OpenClaw update and leave the Codex runtime package behind the main app version, matching the reported `provider is not one of codex` failure evidence. A bare `codex/gpt-*` workaround route could also lose the Codex usage/auth status line because status looked up `codex` instead of the `openai-codex` auth provider.
- Real environment tested: local Windows source worktree on branch `fix/issue-87650-codex-provider-convergence`, commit `734bccac9b`. No live VPS/user install was changed for this PR. The live operator evidence from #88120 is linked/folded into #87650; this PR's after-patch proof is targeted regression/source proof.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts src/status/status-text.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check`
- Evidence after fix: the new doctor regression installs a configured `openai/gpt-5.5` route using the Codex runtime with a stale `@openclaw/codex@2026.5.6` package and verifies doctor refreshes it through the trusted official package update path. A paired regression verifies a newer managed `@openclaw/codex` package is not downgraded. The status regression verifies a `codex/gpt-5.5` Codex harness route queries usage/auth status via `openai-codex` and renders the usage line.
- Observed result after fix: focused doctor/status regression tests passed, adjacent doctor Codex route warning tests passed, formatting check passed, and whitespace diff check passed.
- What was not tested: no live VPS/channel upgrade test was run after this patch. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/update.test.ts` still fails locally on Windows in the existing test `reuses the recorded managed extensions root when updating external plugins`; this PR does not touch `src/plugins/update*`. Direct `codex review --uncommitted` timed out after 10 minutes in this Windows worktree; the bundled helper script also needs bash, which is not available here.

## Verification

Passed locally:

- `src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts`: 62 tests passed.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`: 23 tests passed.
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`: 17 tests passed.
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`: 104 tests passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...`: passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.

Known local proof gap:

- `src/plugins/update.test.ts` has one unrelated local Windows failure in `reuses the recorded managed extensions root when updating external plugins`; no files in that test surface are changed by this PR.
target.author: brokemac79
